title: Siphocampylus glabratus
taxonomy: Siphocampylus glabratus is a species of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae, commonly known as the bellflower family. This family is a large and diverse group of plants that includes around 90 genera and over 2,300 species. Siphocampylus glabratus is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America and is typically found in humid, subtropical environments. The plant is classified as a member of the Campanulaceae family and is often found growing in dense clusters or thickets. The classification of Siphocampylus glabratus reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the family.
